title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Lady Bunty and Miss Marple discuss the upcoming arrival of film star Marina Gregg at Gossington Hall. Lady Bunty mentions learning about Gregg’s private life through salon magazines, though she expresses skepticism regarding rumors that Gregg is an alcoholic or has suffered a mental breakdown. Miss Marple observes that the estate has undergone extensive renovations, including the addition of a swimming pool, a Palm House, and six bathrooms, as well as the conversion of the late Arthur Bantry’s study and library into a music room. Lady Bunty remarks that her late husband, who was tone-deaf, would have disliked these changes.

During their conversation, Lady Bunty suggests that Miss Marple acts like a criminologist, a claim Miss Marple denies, attributing her insights to an understanding of human nature gained from village life. Miss Marple also mentions her interest in a local new development and notes that Miss Knight’s wages were paid by her nephew, Raymond.

After departing via the "Inch" car rental service, Miss Marple visits Miss Knight. Miss Knight informs her that Dr. Huddlestone called and will visit the following morning. When Dr. Huddlestone arrives the next day, he drinks a glass of sherry offered by Miss Marple. He reprimands Miss Marple for a recent fall and mentions that she has been refusing to see Mr. Sandsford, a former partner of the doctor.
